strengths: Single-file implementation keeps the current logic simple and easy to trace.

critical_issues.title: SQL injection via user-controlled query
critical_issues.description: Request parameters flow directly into the SQL statement without any sanitization or parameterization, allowing an attacker to execute arbitrary queries as the database user.
critical_issues.fix: Use a parameterized query (e.g., cursor.execute('SELECT * FROM tasks WHERE owner=?', (user,))) and enforce proper validation of the user identifier.

warnings.title: Missing CRUD surface for tasks
warnings.description: The project claims to offer a full task CRUD experience, but only a GET endpoint is implemented. Neither task creation nor completion updates exist, so the API cannot fulfill its stated responsibilities.
warnings.fix: Add POST/PATCH endpoints to insert and update tasks, wiring them to SQLite and validating payloads before persistence.

recommended_improvements.title: Fix SQL injection
recommended_improvements.howTo: Switch to parameterized queries for all database calls (cursor.execute('SELECT ... WHERE owner=?', (user,))) and validate/normalize input before use.

recommended_improvements.title: Expand CRUD coverage
recommended_improvements.howTo: Implement POST to add tasks and PATCH/PUT to mark tasks complete, each with input validation, transaction handling, and appropriate HTTP response codes.

recommended_improvements.title: Add authentication layer
recommended_improvements.howTo: Integrate JWT middleware that verifies tokens on every request and scopes tasks per authenticated user rather than query parameters.

recommended_improvements.title: Improve SQLite usage
recommended_improvements.howTo: Wrap database access in a context manager (use with sqlite3.connect(...) as conn) to ensure connections close, and move schema initialization into a separate module.

recommended_improvements.title: Document API surface & failure modes
recommended_improvements.howTo: Add docstrings or README entries describing endpoints, expected payloads, and error handling so clients understand how to use the service.

tech_stack_assessment: Python + Flask + SQLite is an appropriate lightweight stack for a simple todo API. To meet the stated goals, the app needs more structured routing/controllers, authentication handling, and schema initialization, but the chosen stack can support those extensions without requiring a rewrite.

inline_annotations.codeSnippet:
user = request.args.get('user', '')
# SQL injection vulnerability — for the model to flag
cursor.execute(f"SELECT * FROM tasks WHERE owner='{user}'")
inline_annotations.title: User input injected into SQL
inline_annotations.message: Interpolating user input directly into SQL allows an attacker to execute arbitrary statements and retrieve or mutate other users' data.
inline_annotations.explanation: SQLite sees the delivered string as executable SQL, so a crafted user value (e.g., "' OR 1=1 --") will bypass authorization entirely.
inline_annotations.suggestedFix: Use parameterized queries and validate the owner identifier before executing database statements.
